name: Rep. Veronica Escobar
tweet: House democrats compromised and passed the senate republican bills to reopen the government. Federal employees are desperate to get back to work, to earn their pay and are depending on these same senate republicans to be the adults in the room. They can end this harmful shutdown

name: Rep. Veronica Escobar
tweet: Finally, legislators should talk to ALL stakeholders here on the border (not JUST law enforcement) in order to truly understand how we can be a better, stronger, more humane nation. 10/10

name: Rep. Veronica Escobar
tweet: 5. Understand the border has never been safer/more secure; border apprehensions are lower today than they were a decade ago; our country has already invested massive and unprecedented amounts of money in “border security”; and, a surge in asylum seekers is not unusual. 9/10

name: Rep. Veronica Escobar
tweet: 4. Return to alternatives to detention. This administration’s approach to migration (criminalization of it and incarceration of migrants) is cruel, ineffective and expensive. 8/10

name: Rep. Veronica Escobar
tweet: 3. Look at what the training, personnel, medical, technology and emergency needs are that would help us adapt to the changes we see on all borders and land and sea ports 7/10

name: Rep. Veronica Escobar
tweet: 2. Evaluate the more than $1 trillion already spent on border security, a wall and sharp increases in personnel in the last two decades and why DHS hasn’t adapted to changing migration patterns and challenges that first arose four years ago. 6/10

name: Rep. Veronica Escobar
tweet: REAL solutions include:  1. Long-term investment and work in Central American countries these many, many families are fleeing. They want to stay at home but cannot because of extreme poverty, persecution and violence.  5/10

name: Rep. Veronica Escobar
tweet: When @SecNielsen was in El Paso 2 wks ago and refused to meet with me and other stakeholders who could’ve helped her better understand the complexities of the humanitarian challenges we face, I knew this administration was uninterested in real solutions. 4/10

name: Rep. Veronica Escobar
tweet: The new challenges on the border have nothing to do with national security. These asylum seekers are running TO (not from) Border Patrol agents, seeking help and refuge. And we have not adapted to families presenting themselves at and between ports of entry. 3/10

name: Rep. Veronica Escobar
tweet: Every time I talk to advocates on the ground, visit a border patrol holding station and speak with agents — my third time in a month — I understand with more clarity that there are serious needs and challenges on our southern border that a wall absolutely will not address. 2/10
